attached_docs: Pasted text(30).txt — KFM GitHub Repository Documentation Implementation Agent v3.1

validation_gates.reason: The merged v1 README was preserved as lineage. The follow-up is limited to the same README plus the required generated-work receipt and does not rewrite merged history.

Main advanced from the v1 merge commit c2224a97e6c87b42edd5a4c07ca9a15a935a8f05 to 69be7dafc6ab7b23961a7d419c384e62bd0c2d22 through an unrelated policy contract README merge. The target retained blob bb9bf99f9b9384bf30a75acab51a4c61a05238d0, and the follow-up branch was created from the newer base.

Existing package placement and document identity were retained. Directory Rules, parent boundaries, prior merged PR, open branch/PR state, contracts, schemas, policy profiles, standards, and drift evidence were inspected without creating parallel authority.

Direct repository evidence still confirms kfm-redaction version 0.0.0, an empty __init__.py, and a comment-only core.py. The expanded README labels all future APIs, transforms, consumers, tests, and operations as proposed, unknown, or needing verification.

v1.1 adds audience, ownership, vocabulary, packaging/import/API, dependency, resource, telemetry, consumer, test-matrix, staged implementation, definition-of-done, verification-register, drift, maintenance, compatibility, correction, and rollback contracts.
The final README has one H1, consecutive heading levels, 59 unique heading slugs, 58 resolved internal anchors, balanced code fences, no trailing whitespace, and a final newline.
All sixteen repository-relative documentation and authority links resolve to files verified at the pinned base.

The change contains no credentials, private source payloads, real living-person or genomic data, exact protected coordinates, transform secrets, or release action. Sensitive examples remain categorical and fail-closed.
GitHub returned README blob 73d0a320b4a08d105aeb3d8822e421c74e356d7f on commit 773a4f9738510fc49dffd5da3d4fb2cccf2066cb, matching the locally recomputed Git blob SHA and SHA-256 artifact hash.

The connector exposes serialized Contents API writes rather than an expected-head atomic multi-file operation with readable base-tree metadata. README and receipt are written sequentially on a scoped review branch with remote read-back after each mutation.
The package remains a placeholder and the API-only workflow did not clone or execute repository code. Pull-request checks are observed separately and are never inferred as passing.
Human review remains pending and is separate from generation, static validation, and mergeability.

emitter: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Thinking via ChatGPT GitHub connector
notes: Task kfm-packages-redaction-namespace-readme-v1-1-20260719. Documentation-only follow-up after merged PR #1424. The receipt records provenance and validation boundaries; it is not factual proof, policy approval, human review, release approval, or publication authority.
